source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, it is known that $ \triangle ABC \cong \triangle DEF $, with B, E, C, F lying on the same straight line. If $ BF = 8\text{cm} $ and $ BE = 2\text{cm} $, then what is the length of $ CE $ in $\text{cm}$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Since $\triangle ABC \cong \triangle DEF$,\\
it follows that $BC = EF$,\\
therefore $BC - CE = EF - CE$,\\
hence $BE = CF$,\\
given that $BE = 2\text{cm}$,\\
it implies $CF = BE = 2\text{cm}$,\\
since $BF = 8\text{cm}$,\\
thus $CE = BF - BE - CF = 8 - 2 - 2 = \boxed{4}\text{cm}$.

Solving CE requires combining at least two geometric relations before the final arithmetic step
The problem is open-response rather than multiple choice, so the numeric result must be derived rather than selected
